utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality regulatory meeting briefings that satisfy all seven evaluation criteria. The wo_skills output is actually larger (25,451 bytes vs 18,844 bytes) and contains more detailed sections with explicit headers for each required element (privilege notes, prior exam context, outstanding items, talking points). However, the wi_skills output demonstrates more sophisticated regulatory-specific framing by explicitly invoking the meeting-briefing skill methodology, using tighter privilege boundary language, and presenting a more strategically cohesive document. The key differentiator is subtle: wi_skills more clearly integrates all elements into a unified enforcement strategy narrative, while wo_skills presents them as well-organized but somewhat independent sections. In this particular test, both outputs score identically on the evaluation rubric, suggesting the baseline agent (wo_skills) was capable enough to independently produce outputs meeting all specified criteria. The skill advantage is present but marginal in this instance, as the baseline agent demonstrated strong regulatory awareness without the skill prompt.

In this particular test case, the quality gap between wi_skills and wo_skills is narrower than the test rationale predicted, as the wo_skills agent performed well above the 'file concatenation' baseline. Nevertheless, wi_skills produced a more formally structured, governance-ready document consistent with its intended board-meeting methodology.
